title: sparganium minimum subvar. septentrionale
intro: Sparganium minimum subvar. septentrionale, also known as dwarf bur-reed, is a rare aquatic plant species that belongs to the family Sparganiaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of dwarf bur-reed can be made by infusing the plant's roots and rhizomes in water or by using it in a decoction, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and wounds.
